evals.prompt: Analyze this file and produce a refactoring plan to apply the nullables pattern: evals/files/hardwired-function.ts
evals.expected_output: Plan identifying both functions as HARDWIRED_INFRASTRUCTURE (standalone functions with OUTSIDE_WORLD side effects) and recommending conversion to an INFRASTRUCTURE_WRAPPER class with DUAL_FACTORY
expectations: Classifies getUserProfile as OUTSIDE_WORLD (it calls fetch)
Classifies saveUserPreferences as OUTSIDE_WORLD (it calls fetch)
Identifies both functions as HARDWIRED_INFRASTRUCTURE
Recommends converting to an INFRASTRUCTURE_WRAPPER class (e.g. UserApiClient) with DUAL_FACTORY (.create/.createNull)
Notes that the pure transformation logic (displayName formatting) can remain separate or inline as PURE code
Recommends an EMBEDDED_STUB for the fetch calls in .createNull
Recommends CONFIGURABLE_RESPONSE for configuring what the nulled client returns

evals.prompt: Analyze this file and produce a refactoring plan to apply the nullables pattern: evals/files/mixed-class.ts
evals.expected_output: Plan identifying ReportGenerator as a class with HARDWIRED_INFRASTRUCTURE that should become a NULLABLE_CLASS, with the I/O extracted into INFRASTRUCTURE_WRAPPERs
expectations: Classifies ReportGenerator as having OUTSIDE_WORLD side effects (fs.readFile, fs.writeFile, fetch)
Identifies the fs and fetch calls as HARDWIRED_INFRASTRUCTURE within the class
Distinguishes the PURE transformation logic (HTML templating, row reduction) from the OUTSIDE_WORLD I/O
Recommends extracting the I/O into one or more INFRASTRUCTURE_WRAPPERs (e.g. FileClient, WebhookClient)
Recommends ReportGenerator become a NULLABLE_CLASS that receives the wrappers via injection
Recommends DUAL_FACTORY on ReportGenerator with .create() instantiating wrappers and .createNull() injecting nulled versions
References the CREATE_BOUNDARY_RULE — wrapper .create() calls should be in the static .create()

evals.prompt: Analyze this file and produce a refactoring plan to apply the nullables pattern: evals/files/partial-nullable.ts
evals.expected_output: Plan identifying CREATE_BOUNDARY_RULE violations in UserRepository where DatabaseClient.create() is called inside instance methods
expectations: Recognizes that UserRepository already has DUAL_FACTORY (.create and .createNull)
Identifies the DatabaseClient.create() call in runMigration() as a CREATE_BOUNDARY_RULE violation
Identifies the DatabaseClient.create() call in syncToAnalytics() as a CREATE_BOUNDARY_RULE violation
Recommends either immediate instantiation (move to static .create) or DELAYED_INSTANTIATION via FACTORY_OBJECT for the migration and analytics database clients
Considers whether DELAYED_INSTANTIATION is appropriate (migration/analytics DBs may only be needed conditionally)
Notes that findUser() correctly uses the injected this.db — no violation there

evals.prompt: Analyze this file and produce a refactoring plan to apply the nullables pattern: evals/files/already-compliant.ts
evals.expected_output: Plan recognizing that the code is already compliant with the nullables pattern, with EmailClient as an INFRASTRUCTURE_WRAPPER and NotificationService as a NULLABLE_CLASS
expectations: Identifies EmailClient as an INFRASTRUCTURE_WRAPPER with DUAL_FACTORY
Identifies NotificationService as a NULLABLE_CLASS with DUAL_FACTORY
Recognizes that CREATE_BOUNDARY_RULE is satisfied — EmailClient.create() is called within NotificationService.create()
Notes that EmailClient has OUTPUT_TRACKING (trackSentEmails)
Reports no major issues — the code is already compliant
Does not recommend unnecessary refactoring
